rules: Every hook must read clearly as an on-screen caption with no audio.
Never write a hook the video doesn't resolve by the payoff beat.
No fake urgency, no guaranteed-outcome language, no fabricated numbers.
Hooks name an observation or a question — they do not make a claim the footage can't back up.

hooks.text: Can you spot what happens before foot strike?
This looks normal until you freeze this frame.
The biggest issue is not where you think it is.
Watch his front leg. Now watch it again.
Something happens here that most coaches miss.
This delivery has one hidden inefficiency.
Freeze it right here. Do you see it?
There's a pattern in this delivery most people scroll past.
This pitch looks clean. Slow it down.
One frame changes how you see this whole delivery.
The mechanics issue isn't in the arm.
hooks.notes: Only use when the video's payoff is genuinely a lower-half or sequencing issue.

hooks.text: Most pitchers never see this moment of their own delivery.

Velocity isn't just about arm speed.

The sequence matters more than the sprint.
This is why two pitchers with the same speed can look completely different.
